title: hard boiled eggs
instructions: Fill a medium pot with 1–2 inches of water and bring it to a boil over high heat.
Using a pasta claw or similar tool, gently lower the eggs into the boiling water one at a time, slowly rolling them in to prevent cracking.
Once all eggs are in, cover the pot and reduce the heat to just above medium (around a '2 o’clock' position on the dial). Maintain a gentle simmer.
With about 1 minute remaining, prepare an ice bath by filling a bowl with ice and adding cold water.
When the eggs are done, use the pasta tool to transfer them directly from the hot water into the ice bath.
Let the eggs sit in the ice bath for at least 25 minutes to fully cool before peeling or storing.
notes: The eggs will often stick up above the water line and steam -- that's fine. If a runnier, molten egg is desired, cook for 9 minutes.
